tools.name: create_document
tools.description: Create a Revise document without a Revise account, seeded with markdown. Returns: url (give this to your user — they open it to view the document and create a free account to keep it, in one step) and edit_token (keep it; use it as your Authorization Bearer token in future requests to read and edit

tools.name: import_document
tools.description: Create a Revise document from a file at a public http(s) URL (.md, .markdown, .txt, .html, .htm, .docx, .rtf, .odt; PDFs/images not yet supported). The server fetches the URL — file bytes are never passed inline. For a LOCAL file, use upload_document instead (it streams the file to the server). Retu

tools.name: upload_document
tools.description: Create a Revise document from a LOCAL file (docx, md, txt, html; PDFs/images not yet supported) without a Revise account and without putting its bytes in your context. Returns a one-time upload_url and a ready-to-run curl command — run the curl to stream the file. The curl response contains url (giv

tools.name: read_document
tools.description: Read a Revise document's content. Use format "markdown" (default) for editing workflows, "text" for plain text. Set with_block_ids to true to interleave <!-- block:xxxxxx --> anchors usable with edit_document. view controls how pending tracked-change suggestions read: "final" (default, as if accepte

tools.name: search_within_document
tools.description: Find where a query matches inside one document. Returns the matching blocks as markdown with <!-- block:xxxxxx --> anchors (usable with edit_document), each with a few neighboring blocks for context. Case-insensitive.

tools.name: get_document_outline
tools.description: Get a document's heading outline with block ids and per-section word counts. Cheap way to orient in a long document before reading specific sections.

tools.name: get_recent_changes
tools.description: See what changed in a document since YOU last looked at it — e.g. edits the human (or another collaborator) made in between. Returns added, removed, and changed blocks (with block ids), all in the accepted/projected state. Great for catching up before continuing work ("how does it look now?"). The f

tools.name: get_document_info
tools.description: Get a document's metadata: title, sharing state, URLs, timestamps, and word count.

tools.name: export_document
tools.description: Export a document by id to markdown, txt, html, docx, pdf. Text formats (markdown, txt, html) are returned inline. Binary formats (docx, pdf) are hosted at a temporary download URL (expires in ~24h) returned in the response, not streamed back inline.

tools.name: edit_document
tools.description: Apply targeted edits to a document using Markdown or HTML content. On a substantial document (20+ words) the default is mode "suggest": edits land as pending tracked changes the owner can accept or reject in the editor. On a short/new document (under 20 words) the default is "apply" (there's little

tools.name: set_page_layout
tools.description: Set a document's page layout: page size, orientation, margins, page numbers, line/paragraph spacing, pageless mode, or the APA-7 preset. Length values are strings with explicit units (e.g. "1in", "2.54cm", "72px").

tools.name: replace_document_content
tools.description: Replace the ENTIRE content of a document with new markdown. Destructive: existing content is removed (it remains recoverable via the document's revision history). Prefer edit_document for targeted changes.

tools.name: insert_footnote
tools.description: Insert a footnote: a numbered superscript reference placed immediately after the anchor text inside a paragraph, plus the footnote body (shown at the bottom of the page, or as an endnote in pageless documents). On substantial documents this lands as a pending tracked change by default, like edit_doc

tools.name: resolve_suggestions
tools.description: Accept or reject the tracked-change suggestions YOU made on a document — only your own, never the user's edits or another agent's suggestions. Use this when the user approves your suggested changes (e.g. they say "lgtm" / "looks good") to apply them on their behalf, or asks you to withdraw them. Cov

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_document" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_document, import_document, upload_document, get_document_info, edit_document · Private data: create_document, import_document, upload_document, read_document, search_within_document, get_document_outline, get_recent_changes, get_document_info, export_document, edit_document, rename_document, set_page_layout, replace_document_content, insert_footnote, resolve_suggestions · Public sink: upload_document · Destructive: upload_document, read_document, insert_footnote.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_document, import_document, upload_document, get_document_info, edit_document · Private data: create_document, import_document, upload_document, read_document, search_within_document, get_document_outline, get_recent_changes, get_document_info, export_document, edit_document, rename_document, set_page_layout, replace_document_content, insert_footnote, resolve_suggestions · Public sink: upload_document · Destructive: upload_document, read_document, insert_footnote

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
